index-BannerSlogan: Welcome to Europe, enjoy your stay.
index-SiteDescription1: Would you like to learn more about europe? Check out the capitals & historical structures!
index-SiteDescription2: Do you feel like improving your geographical skills? Feel free to check out the european maps below!
index-PopularResortsDescription: Would you like to learn more about european history? Check out some of the historical structures listed on our site!
index-CapitalsDescription: Would you like to know more about the heart of european countries and what they can offer for a vacation? We'll provide you with the needed information!
index-GeographicalDescription: This map will show you all of the european countries.
index-PoliticalDescription: Would you like to know some random european statistics? These maps will do it for you.
geographical-BannerSlogan: This geographical map will show you all of the european countries.
political-BannerSlogan: These political maps will show you random statistics about european countries.
political-Covid19MapDescription: Learn more about the sars-cov-2 virus in Europe. The statistics are from the 29th of October 2020.
political-ISSMapDescription: Click here to learn more about how many people per country have visited the international space station.
political-LifeMapDescription: How long do we live for? Click here to learn more about the life expectancy of all countries.
covidMap-BannerSlogan: This map will show you the amount of COVID 19 cases in all european countries.
ISS-BannerSlogan: This map will show you how many people per country have ever visited the ISS.
Life-BannerSlogan: This map will show you how long people usually live for in every country.
capitals-lisbonDescription: Capital of Portugal. The Cathedral of Lisbon and Triumph Arch are the most popular places that tourists visit.
capitals-parisDescription: Capital of France. Also known as the 'City of Love.'
capitals-budapestDescription: Capital of Hungary. Known for surviving 800 years of earthquakes, fires and war without losing its great atmosphere.
capitals-pragueDescription: Capital of the Czech Republic. Being built upon century old architecture from romanian times, this is one of the most popular capitals that Europe has to offer.
capitals-barcelonaDescription: Capital of Spain. Known for being one of the most beautiful europe cities that are next to the beach.
capitals-viennaDescription: Capital of Austria. This city is known for being rich with musical culture.
capitals-berlinDescription: Capital of Germany. Known for being the home of Adolf Hitler and a lot of crazy events during the end of World War 2, this city offers a lot of unique attractions.
capitals-amsterdamDescription: Capital of the Netherlands. This city is known for its citizen who appear to be nice people who prefer to use bicycles rather than cars.
capitals-brusselsDescription: Capital of Belgium. This city offers a lot of chocolate, beers and chips. The Grand-Place which is also known as 'Grote Markt' in dutch grants a great sight to all its ancient buildings.
capitals-sarajevoDescription: Capital of Bosnia. Known for its countless amount of theaters, museums as well as the national museum Herzegovina.
capitals-tiranaDescription: Capital of Albania. Known for having a lot of quad bikes and luxury sedans in its streets.
capitals-athensDescription: Capital of Greece. Athen is known to be the most historical, cultural and economical city of Greece.
capitals-romeDescription: The heart of Italy is known for its old ancient structures that were built during the time of the romans.
capitals-stockholmDescription: Capital of Sweden. This city is known for its 14 islands that are connected with about 50 bridges. It's also famous for the Sankt Nikolai Kyrka.
capitals-warsawDescription: Capital of Poland. It offers a mix of structures such as churches, palaces and building blocks from soviet times. The city had to be rebuilt after its complete destruction during World War 2.
resorts-eiffelDescription: Having a height of 324 meters, the Eiffel Tower is a huge attraction for couples.
resorts-coliseumDescription: Being about 1950 years old, this ancient gladiator arena is an old piece of ancient Rome where they would let prisoners fight for their lives as gladiators in action packed battles between life and death as entertainment for their citizen. If a gladiator was victorious in battle, then he would be freed.
resorts-castleItterDescription: Austrian Castle from the 19th Century that holds a special kind of story from World War 2. In 1943, during World War 2, it was turned into a Nazi prison for french VIPs. On 4 May 1945, the U.S. Army, the Austrian Resistance, the German Wehrmacht, the prisoners themselves and a defected Waffen-SS officer fought side-by-side against the Waffen-SS in the 'Battle for Castle Itter' prior to the end of the war. The defense was led by U.S. Captain John C. 'Jack' Lee and Wehrmacht Major Josef 'Sepp' Gangl. Together the four groups made of americans including 2 Sherman tanks, germans, austrians and french prisonders managed to repell forces of the Waffen-SS throughout the night. The battle continued throughout the morning of 5 May, with a strong force of about 150 Waffen-SS soldiers pressing the attack until reinforcements from the American 142nd Infantry Regiment arrived around 4 PM that day. Even though they were victorious, at that day Wehrmacht Major Josef ‘Sepp’ Gangl had lost his life. He was shot by a sniper while helping a french prisoner get away from harm. After the war, fell into disrepair until 1950 when Willi Woldrich acquired it and turned it into a luxury hotel. But the hotel encountered financial problems and it was acquired by a holding company before it was sold to a private owner called Dr. Ernst Bosin in 1985. Since that time it is no longer open to the public.
resorts-bunkerDescription: One of the most iconic attractions in Germany. Constructions started in 1943 and were completed in 1944. The Führerbunker is known for some horrible events that took place a few days before the war ended. On 29 April 1945, Adolf Hitler doubted that the cyanide capsules would work properly and tested them on his dog Blondi to verify the capsule's contents. She died as a result. Due to that, Hitler became inconsolable and on 30 April 1945, he married Eva Braun and then they commited suicide. Braun took a cyanide capsule and Hitler shot himself. On the same day Fritz Tornow, Hitler's dog handler and who was upset after he was forced to assist in killing Blondi, took and shot Blondi's pups, Eva Braun's two dogs, Gerda Christian's dogs and his own dachshund in the garden of the bunker which upset him even further. A few days later Joseph Goebbels and his wife Magda Goebbels killed all of their six children and themselves. One of their children had indicators of a fight between her and her attacker. On 2 May 1945, the soviets took control of the bunker and only 5 people were alive: Fritz Tornow, Werner Haase, Erna Flegel, Lisolette Chervinska & Johannes Hentschel.
resorts-catacombsDescription: The catacombs of Paris were built in 1810 when the cemetries were overflowing and caused a shortage of space to bury the dead. Being about 20 meters deep in the ground, he catacombs hold the remains of about 6 million people and are stretching beneath the whole street system of Paris. The catacombs hold gruesome stories about explorers who lost themselves in the endless labyrinth with walls made out of human bones.
